title: Nasdaq 100 Rallies, Oil Sinks To $75 On Iran Peace Deal: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. stocks rebounded Thursday with the Nasdaq 100 leading gains on a semiconductor rally fueled by an Intel-Apple chip manufacturing partnership. Oil prices tumbled 2.2% to $75/barrel following a U.S.-Iran peace deal and Strait of Hormuz reopening. The S&P 500 rose 1.2% while the Russell 2000 fell 0.7% amid mixed market performance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Surged 9.8% following announcement of Apple partnership to design and build chips in America, extending its strong 400%+ run since 2025 government investment
Benefited from semiconductor sector rally and positive sentiment from Intel partnership announcement, though modest 0.49% gain reflects broader tech strength
Rose 3.9% in sympathy with Intel's surge and broader semiconductor sector strength
Gained 4.24% as semiconductor peers rallied on Intel-Apple deal and AI-silicon momentum
Climbed 12.1% riding the AI-silicon rally and semiconductor sector strength
Added 11.5% benefiting from AI-silicon rally and semiconductor sector momentum
Rose 11.0% on strength in memory pricing within the semiconductor sector
Plunged 17.4% after cutting fiscal-year revenue-growth guidance due to cautious enterprise spending and slowdown in U.S. federal business
Fell 10.4% on Accenture's negative guidance read-across affecting the entire digital-consulting cohort
Dropped 9.9% due to Accenture's cautious outlook impacting the consulting sector
Lost 9.8% as consulting sector repriced following Accenture's revenue guidance cut
Slid 8.7% on Accenture's negative read-across to the IT services and consulting industry

title: Astera Labs Stock Is Under Pressure: What's Happening Today?
author: Evette Mitkov
description: Astera Labs (ALAB) shares declined 5.13% on Tuesday, pulling back after a strong Monday rally driven by U.S.-Iran peace agreement news that boosted semiconductor stocks. Despite the pullback, the stock remains in a strong uptrend with technical indicators showing overbought conditions (RSI at 70.35), suggesting potential for further consolidation or short-term volatility.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the stock experienced a 5.13% pullback on Tuesday, the article emphasizes the stock's strong underlying uptrend (trading 12.7% above 20-day MA, 50% above 50-day MA, with a golden cross formation). The pullback is characterized as normal profit-taking after an exceptional run (4x gain over 12 months) and overbought technical conditions (RSI 70.35), rather than a fundamental deterioration. The sentiment is neutral as the article presents both the pullback and the constructive longer-term structure.

title: Insiders Sell Top Tech Plays: Should Investors Buy, Sell, or Hold?
author: Thomas Hughes
description: Heavy insider selling across tech stocks raises questions about whether it signals a warning or routine profit-taking. Analysis of NuScale Power, Astera Labs, and Dell shows that while insiders are selling, institutional buying, bullish analyst sentiment, and strong business catalysts suggest these stocks may still have upside potential despite the insider activity.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite heavy insider selling in Q1-Q2, institutional ownership is nearly 80% with strong quarterly buying. Bullish long-term outlook as small modular reactor play with Federal support, Trump administration backing for space-based nuclear power by 2028, and TVA power purchase agreement catalyst. Consensus price target indicates ~50% upside.

Aggressive insider selling offset by 300% annual gains and tripled stock price since April 2026, indicating profit-taking. Institutional buying at 3-to-1 pace driven by critical AI data center connectivity products. Strong catalysts include new product launches and inference boom, though stock is 40% above consensus target and may face near-term correction.

Insider selling limited to Silver Lake Partners taking profits from 2026 rally. Institutions (excluding Silver Lake) own 70%+ and accumulated shares over multiple quarters. Strong business fundamentals with AI-optimized servers in high demand. Analyst price target of $700 suggests 70% upside. Fiscal Q2 2027 results expected in September with forecasted 50%+ revenue growth.

Mentioned as key catalyst for Dell and broader AI infrastructure demand. Upcoming results expected to show continued sequential and year-over-year acceleration tied to AI GPU demand, providing impetus for related tech stocks.

title: Nasdaq-100 Index® June 2026 Quarterly Changes
author: Nasdaq
description: Nasdaq announced the results of its June 2026 quarterly rebalance of the Nasdaq-100 Index, effective June 22, 2026. Five companies were added to the index: Astera Labs, CoreWeave, Nebius Group, Rocket Lab, and Teradyne. Five companies were removed: Charter Communications, Cognizant Technology Solutions, Insmed, Verisk Analytics, and Zscaler.

title: Looking Beyond Semiconductors? These 8 Tech Stocks Offer Up to 60% Upside
author: David Wagner
description: US semiconductor stocks rebounded strongly on Monday after a sharp selloff, with Intel, Micron, and Marvell among the biggest gainers. However, despite the recovery, these semiconductor stocks remain significantly overvalued according to Fair Value estimates. The article highlights alternative large-cap tech stocks trading at meaningful discounts (35.5%-60.5%) with strong growth prospects, including Futu Holdings and Leidos Holdings, which offer more attractive valuations than the crowded semiconductor sector.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite Monday's 11.2% rebound, Intel carries 44.5% downside potential according to Fair Value estimates, indicating significant overvaluation despite AI inference optimism.
While rebounding 9.9% on Monday, Micron shows 25.8% downside potential based on Fair Value estimates, suggesting overvaluation in the semiconductor sector.
Despite 9.6% gains and S&P 500 inclusion support, Marvell carries 38% downside potential according to Fair Value estimates, indicating overvaluation.
Rebounded 9.3% but shows 36.6% downside potential based on Fair Value estimates, reflecting overvaluation in the semiconductor equipment sector.
Gained 8.6% on Monday but carries 34.2% downside potential according to Fair Value estimates, indicating overvaluation despite sector rebound.
Rose 9.2% but shows 41.3% downside potential based on Fair Value estimates, suggesting significant overvaluation relative to fundamentals.
Trades at attractive valuation with trailing P/E of 10.5 well below historical average, 24.7% YoY revenue growth, and 35.5%-60.5% upside potential based on Fair Value and analyst targets.
Down 30% in 2026 despite steady fundamentals, 4% YoY revenue growth, 5% EPS growth, raised full-year outlook, and offers 35.5%-60.5% upside potential with meaningful valuation discount.
